evals.name: backend-feature-invitation-expiry
evals.prompt: I need to add expiry to closed sign-up invitations in our backend. Right now invitations live forever in the `invitations` table — once an admin creates one, it stays valid until consumed. We want admins to be able to set a TTL when creating an invitation (default 7 days, max 30, configurable per-row), and the auth pre-create hook should reject expired invitations with a clear error. Also: a daily background job that purges invitations expired more than 30 days ago. Backend only — frontend changes are a separate task. Use the workflow.

assertions.text: Output contains a work-doc.md file with YAML frontmatter including slug, title, status, type=feature, project
Work-doc has a 'Clarifying Q&A' section with at least 3 numbered questions
Work-doc has a 'Definition of Done' section with at least 5 verifiable bullets
Work-doc has a 'Tasks' section with at least 4 bite-sized checkbox tasks
Tasks include both implementation AND test tasks (each business-logic task paired with a test)
Proposed implementation describes a migration adding an `expires_at` column to the invitations table
Proposed implementation describes changes to the invitations service (TTL accept, default 7d, max 30d)
Proposed implementation describes changes to the auth pre-create hook (rejects expired invitations with a clear error)
Proposed implementation describes a cron/scheduled job for purging invitations expired more than 30 days ago
Transcript shows clarify -> plan -> gate -> implement -> verify -> finish flow (or explicitly notes deviations)
Transcript references the simulated user-approval at the Phase 2 gate
Self-review checklist is filled in within the work-doc Verification section

evals.name: frontend-redesign-team-toolbar
evals.prompt: The team page toolbar in our frontend is cluttered — the Invite, Filter, and Search controls don't have a clear hierarchy and the spacing feels arbitrary. I want it tightened to fit a clear visual direction. Keep the same controls; nothing functional changes. Use the workflow.

assertions.text: Transcript explicitly mentions loading references/frontend-design.md (recognizing the FE-design trigger)
Work-doc clarifying questions cover: visual direction / what stays / what changes / brand or design system applies
Work-doc Approach section explicitly names the committed visual direction
Work-doc DoD includes a 'no new generic-AI signals' or equivalent bullet (forbids stock AI-UI palettes / fonts)
Test mode for the implementation tasks is 'manual smoke' (cosmetic-only)
Smoke steps include RTL toggle / mobile breakpoint / dark mode if relevant
Proposed implementation does NOT introduce new font families without explicit reason
Proposed implementation uses design tokens / CSS variables / utility classes — not inline hex colors or px paddings outside the system
Proposed implementation uses logical properties (margin-inline-start / padding-inline-end) for any spacing changes if RTL is in scope
Transcript shows the visual quality gate (5 questions: clear point of view, intentional typography/spacing, supportive color/motion, avoids AI-UI feel, production-grade)
Work-doc has YAML frontmatter with type=redesign or type=revamp
Self-review checklist filled in within the work-doc Verification section

evals.name: bug-fix-401-on-tenant-route
evals.prompt: We have a bug in the backend. An integration test for the GET /api/invitations endpoint is failing with `expected 200, got 401` when called against a tenant subdomain. It started failing after we merged a multi-tenant auth refactor last week. I already tried adding `await` to one of the auth handlers — didn't help. Find the root cause and fix it. Use the workflow.

assertions.text: Transcript recognizes this as a debug task (Phase 3b / 4-phase systematic debug)
Work-doc clarifying questions focus on reproduction + recent changes + scope (matches the `fix` or `debug` question bank)
Phase D1 (root cause) — work-doc/transcript shows evidence-gathering at component boundaries (tenant resolver, auth middleware, route handler, DB query, etc.)
Phase D2 (pattern analysis) — work-doc/transcript references comparing against a working analogue (another tenant route)
Phase D3 — at least one hypothesis is stated explicitly in the form 'I think X is the root cause because Y'
Phase D4 — proposed fix includes a failing regression test that fails before the fix and passes after (red-green cycle)
Transcript honors the 3-hypothesis circuit breaker (mentions stopping if 3 fail and surfacing architectural concern)
The user's earlier `await` fix attempt is recognized as a symptom-level patch and either reverted or technically justified
Proposed fix targets the SOURCE of the bug, not the symptom
DoD includes 'no other tests broken' / 'all tests still pass'
Work-doc YAML frontmatter has type=fix or type=debug
Self-review checklist filled in within the work-doc Verification section
